system: two coupled LC resonator flux coordinates with one observed node

practical: node voltage on resonator 1 integrated or phase-referenced to infer flux_phi1

regime_assumptions: linear inductive coupling is a valid quadratic energy model
the visible block H_vv is declared as the ceiling for hidden load
flux coordinates are the declared energy coordinates
equation_hook: U = flux_phi1^2/(2 L1) + flux_phi2^2/(2 L2) + (flux_phi1 - flux_phi2)^2/(2 Lc)
coordinate_note: Both active coordinates are flux coordinates in the same units; this card does not mix charge and flux blocks.

module_object.description: 2x2 inductive energy Hessian with one visible and one hidden flux coordinate

derivation_status: exact after declaring the linear coupled-flux energy model

ceiling_or_reference.description: T = H_vv, the visible flux stiffness before hidden relaxation

positive_scope: This card exposes the same hidden-renormalised visible stiffness story as coupled springs, now in a circuit substrate.
boundary: It does not describe nonlinear coupling, dissipative linewidths, drive response, or measurement back-action.
failure_mode: The attachment fails if the measured resonator cannot be approximated by the declared passive linear quadratic energy.
next_measurement: Sweep coupling or detuning and compare the inferred visible stiffness at node 1 with the declared hidden-load prediction.
